skill.name: e2e-testing-patterns
skill.description: Guides teams through practical Playwright and Cypress patterns for reliable end-to-end tests, CI execution, selectors, fixtures, mocking, accessibility checks, and flake debugging.
skill.summary: Build reliable Playwright and Cypress end-to-end test suites with practical patterns.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported command execution, network access, environment access, weak cryptography, and a critical combined heuristic. Review found documentation examples for Playwright and Cypress, including fenced code samples, localhost URLs, CI environment variables, and test runner commands. No prompt injection, malicious intent, credential exfiltration, or executable skill script was found.

low_findings.title: Documentation Code Fences Misidentified as Command Execution
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The flagged locations are Markdown code fences, TypeScript examples, and documented Playwright CLI examples. They are not executable skill code or hidden automation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The inspected locations are rendered documentation, not script files. The only concrete commands are test runner examples for headed and debug Playwright execution.

low_findings.title: Local Test Networking Examples
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The network findings are local base URLs and Playwright or Cypress API mocking examples. No external exfiltration endpoint or unauthorized remote service was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs and request calls are part of standard E2E testing examples. They target localhost or relative application routes, with no credential transmission to third parties.

low_findings.title: Environment Variable Examples for CI and Test Credentials
low_findings.description: Verdict: LOW_RISK_TRUE_POSITIVE. The documentation shows environment variable use for CI behavior and an admin test password. This is expected in test configuration, but users should avoid exposing real secrets in test artifacts.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The environment access is explicit and semantically legitimate for CI and test credentials. The risk is limited because no file read, logging, network exfiltration, or executable skill hook is present.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography and Reconnaissance Scanner Matches Are Contextual False Positives
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The flagged lines refer to browser device names, response matching, data cleanup, selectors, and general testing guidance. No weak cryptographic algorithm or system reconnaissance behavior was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found no cryptographic code, host probing, fingerprinting, or environment enumeration. The alerts appear to be substring matches inside normal testing prose and sample code.

low_findings.title: Critical Combined Heuristic Dismissed After Semantic Review
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The combination of code blocks, local network test examples, and environment variables triggered a critical heuristic. The skill contains documentation only and no malicious execution path.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The underlying signals are legitimate E2E testing examples. I found no prompt injection, encoded payloads, credential collection flow, or unauthorized outbound target.

content.value_statement: End-to-end tests often become slow, flaky, and hard to maintain. This skill provides practical Playwright and Cypress patterns for stable workflows, CI runs, selectors, fixtures, and debugging.
actual_capabilities: Explains when to use end-to-end tests versus unit or integration tests.
Provides Playwright setup patterns for retries, reporters, projects, and trace capture.
Shows Page Object, fixture, waiting, and network mocking patterns.
Provides Cypress configuration, custom command, request, and intercept examples.
Covers visual regression, accessibility scanning, parallel execution, and debugging workflows.
Includes selector guidance and common pitfalls for reducing flaky tests.
limitations: It is guidance documentation, not a runnable test generator.
Examples require adaptation to each application, framework, and test data model.
It does not provide complete project-specific CI configuration.
It cannot guarantee test stability without stable environments and isolated data.

use_cases.title: Define a Critical Journey Test Plan
use_cases.description: Map high-value user flows into focused Playwright or Cypress tests with stable selectors and clear assertions.

use_cases.title: Reduce Flaky Browser Tests
use_cases.description: Replace fixed waits, brittle selectors, and shared state with deterministic waits, fixtures, cleanup, and trace artifacts.

use_cases.title: Standardize CI Browser Testing
use_cases.description: Plan retries, parallel execution, reports, screenshots, videos, and accessibility checks for pull request pipelines.

prompt_templates.title: Choose What to Test
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this feature and identify the best end-to-end test cases. Focus on critical user journeys and avoid unit-level details.

prompt_templates.title: Create Stable Selectors
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me design stable selectors and assertions for this page. Prefer accessible roles, labels, and test IDs where appropriate.

prompt_templates.title: Debug Flaky Tests
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this flaky end-to-end test behavior. Suggest changes to waits, data setup, cleanup, retries, and artifact capture.

prompt_templates.title: Design a CI E2E Strategy
prompt_templates.prompt: Design an end-to-end testing strategy for this repository. Include Playwright or Cypress structure, CI parallelization, reports, and failure triage.

output_examples.input: Plan tests for a checkout flow.
output: A focused list of critical checkout journeys.
Recommended assertions for cart, payment, confirmation, and error states.
Suggested fixture and cleanup strategy for test users and orders.
A diagnosis of likely timing, selector, and state issues.
Replacement waits based on page state and visible outcomes.
Artifact recommendations for traces, screenshots, and retry evidence.

output_examples.input: Add accessibility checks to E2E tests.
output: A plan for where accessibility scans add value.
Guidance for excluding known third-party widgets.
A review workflow for violations found during CI.

best_practices: Test user-visible behavior and critical journeys before lower-value edge cases.
Use stable selectors, isolated data, deterministic waits, and cleanup for each test.
Capture traces, screenshots, videos, and reports in CI for faster failure diagnosis.
anti_patterns: Do not use fixed sleep timers when page state or network events can be observed.
Do not depend on CSS class chains, DOM positions, or shared test order.
Do not run destructive tests against production systems or real customer data.

faq.question: Does this skill support both Playwright and Cypress?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes patterns and examples for both Playwright and Cypress end-to-end testing.

faq.question: Can it generate a complete test suite automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides implementation guidance that must be adapted to the application under test.

faq.question: Does it cover flaky test debugging?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers waits, selectors, isolation, retries, traces, screenshots, and video artifacts.

faq.question: Is this skill useful for CI pipelines?
faq.answer: Yes. It discusses retries, parallel execution, sharding, reports, and artifact capture for CI.

faq.question: Does it include accessibility testing guidance?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes examples for accessibility scans with Playwright and axe.

faq.question: Should teams use it for unit tests?
faq.answer: No. The skill focuses on browser-level workflows. Unit and integration tests need different patterns.
